source_report_section: Test 1: Can models distinguish real vulnerabilities from false positives?
description: REPORT-2 uses a short Java servlet from the OWASP benchmark that looks like textbook SQL injection, but remove(0) discards the user-controlled value before get(1) feeds a constant string into the SQL query. The correct answer is that the snippet is not currently vulnerable, even though it is fragile and easy to misread.

source_report_section: [April 9, 2026]: Update can models tell when a bug has been fixed?
description: REPORT-2 includes a specificity check that reruns the same FreeBSD RPCSEC_GSS function after the bounds-checking patch has been applied. The correct answer for this control is that the patched function is safe and should not be reported as still vulnerable.
